title: Add tyre-cliff and change-point features instead of simple rolling lags
summary: Public tyre modeling references emphasize nonlinear end-of-stint loss, thermal fade, graining, and flat-spot step changes rather than smooth linear degradation.
rationale: Public tyre modeling references emphasize nonlinear end-of-stint loss, thermal fade, graining, and flat-spot step changes rather than smooth linear degradation. The worst local solutions already tried generic lag/rolling features and underperformed, which suggests the useful signal is likely in curvature or abrupt regime shifts relative to the field, not raw rolling means.
implementation_hint: Within each driver-race-stint, compute past-only slope and acceleration of `LapTime_Delta`, `LapTime (s)`, and `Cumulative_Degradation`; add deviation from race-lap median slope, cumulative positive-delta streak length, recent max deterioration, and change-point flags when pace loss jumps beyond compound/race-normal bands. Interact these with `TyreLife`, absolute compound, and `Position` to separate genuine tyre cliffs from traffic noise.
expected_effect: Could recover last-lap-before-pit behavior that current static feasibility windows and naive lags miss, especially on high-deg circuits.
risk: Very sensitive to noisy laps, traffic, and safety-car contamination; requires careful past-only construction and robust clipping.
